description: Generators must follow dispatch instructions issued by AEMO and operate within their dispatch targets

description: Generators must meet registered performance standards for frequency, voltage, and reactive power

description: Metering data providers must collect and deliver metering data within prescribed timeframes

description: Generators must contribute to frequency control ancillary services as registered

description: TNSPs and generators must coordinate planned outages through the Network Outage Scheduler

description: Market participants must respond to market notices and directions from AEMO within specified timeframes

description: Large customers and retailers must provide demand forecasts to AEMO

description: Participants must verify settlement data and raise disputes within prescribed periods

description: All participants must maintain current registration details and classifications with AEMO

source_legislation: Competition and Consumer Act CDR Rules
description: Energy retailers and distributors must provide consumer data on request

obligation_name: Consumer Data Right — Energy Data Sharing
source_legislation: Consumer Data Right Act 2019 — Energy Sector Rules
description: Energy retailers and networks must securely expose customer energy data via accredited CDR APIs on request
key_requirements: Implement CDR API v3.0+; Consent management; Data quality validation; Monthly compliance attestation; Register with ACCC CDR

description: Large retailers (>100 GWh) must contract sufficient reliability instruments to cover peak demand periods
key_requirements: Procure reliability instruments pre-peak; Quarterly net contract position reports; Notify AER of shortfall; Comply with RRO trigger mechanism

description: Distributors must ensure distributed energy resources (solar, batteries, EVs) comply with AS/NZS 4777 and AEMO DER technical settings
key_requirements: Register DER assets; Enforce technical settings at connection; Annual DER audit; Report non-compliant devices; Update DER register

source_legislation: NEL Section 88 — AEMC Rule Change Process
description: Affected market participants must engage with AEMC rule change consultations that materially affect their operations
key_requirements: Monitor AEMC rule change register; Submit formal responses to material changes; Attend open consultations; Track implementation timelines

description: Electricity retailers in regulated regions must not charge standing offer customers above the DMO reference price
key_requirements: Publish DMO-compliant standing offers; Annual price review; AER notification 10 days before changes; Customer communication; Record-keeping 3 years

description: Covered facilities (>100 kt CO2-e) must surrender eligible carbon credits or face penalties if emissions exceed baseline

description: All metering installations and market participants must comply with five-minute settlement dispatch and metering requirements

description: Network service providers must maintain structural, legal and accounting separation from related electricity businesses
key_requirements: Separate accounts; Independent board members; No cross-subsidisation; Annual ring-fencing compliance report; Notify AER of related-party transactions
